milestone: My Cloud Worlds — Card Layout Fix
slice: A real, user-supplied screenshot of the just-shipped My Cloud Worlds feature showed every card's World Name crushed to 1-2 characters, overlapping the action button. Two compounding CSS bugs fixed. (1) .wb-cloud-world-card inherited the 112px right padding .wb-project-card reserves for My World Projects' own absolutely-positioned Rename/Duplicate/Delete controls -- a Cloud World card has none, so that padding sat empty, wasting real horizontal space; fixed with the same padding-right:18px override .wb-project-card-repo-only already uses for the identical reason. (2) Even with that space reclaimed, the card's own action is a real text label ('Sync to This Device' / 'Update Available -- Load It'), not a single glyph like Rename/Duplicate/Delete -- it can't share one narrow inline flex row with a long World Name without the name still getting crushed, since the flex-shrink:0 button refuses to shrink and all the squeeze falls onto the name. Fixed by restructuring _cloudWorldCard() into a two-row stack -- a new .wb-cloud-world-top (thumb+info, a nested row) sits on its own line, with the action button now a full-width row beneath it. Verified via a direct Playwright measurement against the exact realistic long name from the reported screenshot: before the fix, the name's rendered box measured 64.6px wide (crushed to a couple characters) with the button crowding right beside it; after, the name box measures 224px wide (the full name renders, no crushing) with the button on its own 290px-wide row beneath, zero overlap. Both goldenBuild.js suites and all three existing Node/Playwright verification suites for this feature (13/13, 15/15, 8/8, all zero console errors) pass unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to tools/world-builder-v2/css/world-builder.css and tools/world-builder-v2/js/worldBuilderApp.js.
